message:
test(frankenterm-core): swap recorder migration tests from mocks to real append-log storage

Migrates `recorder_migration_labruntime.rs` from a mock-storage harness
(MockStorage with custom AppendRequest/AppendResponse capture, mocked
checkpoint commit + lag tracking) to the real AppendLogRecorderStorage
backed by a tempdir. Same test surface — 26 tests in, 26 tests out —
but the assertions now exercise the actual on-disk append-log format
and the real flush/checkpoint codepaths instead of mocked stubs.

Why the rewrite:

The MockStorage stand-in was diverging from the real backend's
behaviour. Issues that motivated the swap include:
- Mock didn't model the WAL flush boundary, so any test that
  inadvertently relied on durability ordering would pass under the
  mock and silently regress against real storage.
- Mock's CheckpointCommitOutcome bookkeeping was a hand-rolled
  HashMap<&str, u64> approximation rather than the engine's actual
  outcome tagging — a source of low-fidelity assertions.
- Tests could not catch wire-format drift between
  AppendLogStorageConfig defaults and what the engine actually wrote
  out, because the mock never serialised anything.

What changed in this file:

- Drop the MockStorage / MockReader scaffolding and the
  AppendResponse / RecorderConsumerLag / RecorderStorageHealth /
  RecorderStorageLag mock-side imports.
- Bring in `AppendLogRecorderStorage`, `AppendLogStorageConfig`,
  `tempfile::{TempDir, tempdir}`, and `std::fs::File / io::Read /
  path::PathBuf` for real-disk fixture wiring.
- Bring in `RuntimeFixture` from the existing common::fixtures module
  to reuse the lab-runtime setup the rest of the recorder test suite
  uses (gives the test a consistent runtime + tempdir lifecycle).
- New helpers `read_append_log_records(...)` and
  `head_offset_for_records(records)` to slurp the on-disk records and
  derive the head offset for assertions, replacing what the mock used
  to expose via captured AppendRequest vectors.
- 26 test bodies migrated; each now constructs a tempdir-backed
  AppendLogRecorderStorage instead of MockStorage and asserts against
  the on-disk records produced by the engine.

This is part of the broader push to swap the recorder test suite from
mocks to real lab-runtime + real append-log fixtures, in line with the
no-mocks testing posture for end-to-end conformance coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
